title: Hugging Face's CEO on Open Source AI, Model Routing, and the Future of Competition
summary: As governments weigh new restrictions on frontier AI models, one question is becoming increasingly important: what role should open source play in the future of artificial intelligence? Theo Jaffee and Sofia Puccini speak with Hugging Face CEO Clément Delangue about AI regulation, open source safety, model routing, and why he believes competition—not consolidation—is essential for the industry's future. They discuss GPT-5, government oversight of frontier models, Hugging Face surpassing $100 million in annual recurring revenue, local AI, China's open-source ecosystem, Europe's AI ambitions,...
score_reason: Strong content potential with a clear practitioner perspective on open source AI, regulation, and competition, offering insight and trend read value.
partner_takeaway: Open source AI and competition are critical factors shaping the future of AI regulation and industry dynamics.
partner_summary: Hugging Face's CEO discusses the evolving role of open source in AI, model routing, and the importance of competition over consolidation. This matters to AI practitioners and business leaders navigating regulatory changes and strategic positioning. The audience should monitor open source developments and consider how competition influences AI innovation and safety.

title: Who’s Afraid of Chinese Models?
